name: authority_cleanup_provider_runtime
outcome_goal: Move integration discoverability, workflow discovery, and provider operational defaults onto seeded DB authority so runtime selection no longer depends on startup syncs or hardcoded default maps.
anti_requirements: Do not keep Python registry sync as the source of truth.
Do not leave filesystem queue scanning in place for runtime discovery.
Do not preserve hardcoded context-window or probe-model maps as canonical behavior.

jobs.label: provider_runtime_packet
jobs.outcome_goal: Cut integration/discovery/provider selection over to DB-backed runtime authority.
jobs.anti_vibe_rule: No duplicate seeds, no mirrored truth, no queue-file discovery masquerading as runtime catalog.
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE:
Move integration, discovery, and provider operational defaults onto DB authority.

TASK:
Make `integration_registry` and the seeded app-builder registries authoritative for runtime discoverability, remove startup sync/bootstrap writes as runtime truth, stop using filesystem queue scanning for runtime workflow discovery, and resolve provider context/probe defaults from admitted DB rows and bindings.

CONSTRAINTS:
Keep code-owned loader implementations only where they are pure implementation. Do not widen into dispatch resolver or verifier contract work. Preserve idempotent migration/backfill behavior for partially seeded databases.

OUTPUT_CONTRACT:
Ship the migrations, runtime changes, and tests needed so integration selection, workflow discovery, and provider defaults are DB-backed and explicitly seeded.

ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA:
- `integration_registry` is the discoverability and action-identity source.
- Runtime startup no longer writes duplicate registry seed data as authority.
- Chat/operator workflow listing no longer scans queue files as runtime truth.
- Provider context-window and probe selection derive from DB bindings.

STOP_BOUNDARY:
Do not rework UI routing or rebuild the dispatch frontdoor in this packet.
